description_long: This object consists of six black traffic light housings, each containing three circular signal lights arranged vertically. The lights display standard traffic signal colors—red, yellow, and green—with some lenses featuring directional arrows. The housings are robust and angular, with protective visors extending over each light to shield from sunlight and weather. The lenses are made of colored glass or plastic, and the main body appears to be constructed from durable metal or hard plastic. The asset also includes six detached circular signal lenses, shown separately to the side, highlighting the internal components. The overall design is typical for urban intersections, providing clear visual signals for vehicle control.
description: A set of six black, vertically-stacked traffic lights with colored signal lenses and directional arrows, accompanied by six detached signal covers. The housings are sturdy and feature protective visors over each light.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six traffic lights with visible colored lenses and arrows, plus six detached signal covers.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the depth of the housings and the protruding visors over each signal light.
description_view_2: Rear/top-down view showing the backs of the housings and the flat, featureless surfaces, with detached covers to the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the cylindrical protrusions of the signal lights and the thickness of the housings.
